name: cumulative

metadata.name: cumulative
metadata.summary: Manipulate and Visualize Time Series Collections

documentation:
---

Manipulate and Visualize Time Series Collections

An open-source Python library for Data Scientists to efficiently manipulate collections of time series data. Features include data loading, transformation, persistence, and visualization. --- * **Documentation**: [https://elehcimd.github.io/cumulative](https://elehcimd.github.io/cumulative) * **Source code**: [https://github.com/elehcimd/cumulative](https://github.com/elehcimd/cumulative) (License: [BSD 3-Clause](https://elehcimd.github.io/cumulative/license/)) * **Discussions**: [Ask questions, share ideas, engage](https://github.com/elehcimd/cumulative/discussions) * **Funding**: You can [star](https://github.com/elehcimd/cumulative) the project on GitHub and [hire me](https://www.linkedin.com/in/dallachiesa/) to make sense of your time series. ---

functions.name: cumulative.animation.fig_to_image
documentation:
Given a figure, return it as an image in PNG format, with
tight layout and no padding.

functions.name: cumulative.animation.ax_func_to_figure
documentation:
Given a function that returns a matplotlib axes,
returns it as a PNG image (bytes).

classes.name: cumulative.animation.Animation

methods.name: cumulative.animation.Animation.plot_ctx
documentation:
Return a context manager generator for the plot context, setting
some options to their correct values for animations.

methods.name: cumulative.animation.Animation.clear
documentation: Clear all animation frames.

methods.name: cumulative.animation.Animation.add_frame
documentation: Add `ax` as a new frame of the animation.

methods.name: cumulative.animation.Animation.add_frame_func
documentation:
Add a frame of the animation as a function that returns a matplotlib.axes.Axes object.
The function is not blocking, and its evaluation might be parallelized in .render().

methods.name: cumulative.animation.Animation.render
documentation:
Render frames of animation and store them. Function frames are generated
in parallel by default. You can control the degree of parallelization with `n_jobs`,
passed to joblib.

methods.name: cumulative.animation.Animation.show
documentation: Show animation as an HTML5 video with iPython.

methods.name: cumulative.animation.Animation.show_gif
documentation: Show animation as an embedded GIF.

methods.name: cumulative.animation.Animation.save
documentation: Save animation in MP4 format.

Helper class that simplifies the creation of animations.
The generation of frames is parallelized by default, for
frames passed by .add_frame_func(...).

functions.name: cumulative.loaders.preprocess.melt
documentation:
Unpivot dataframe `dt` from wide to long format. Similar to Pandas.melt, but more flexible.
`pattern` controls which columns provide numerical values, and instructs how to extract them.
`attributes` lists the columns to be retained as attributes of the series (not necessarily numeric).

functions.name: cumulative.loaders.preprocess.nest
documentation:
Transform series in long format to nested format. Expected columns:
- "name": name of the series
- "c": value of the columns representing x
- "x": extracted x value from "c"
- "y": value at position "x"
- "attr.*": attributes associated to the series (optional)

functions.name: cumulative.loaders.preprocess.nest_xy
documentation:
Transform series in long format to nested format. Expected columns:
- `name`: name of the series
- `y`: value at a certain offset

The X column will be deduced by the offset of Y.

methods.name: cumulative.cumulative.Cumulative.__init__
documentation: Instantiate a new Cumulative interface to dataframe `df`.

methods.name: cumulative.cumulative.Cumulative.dup
documentation: Returns a deep copy of the object.

methods.name: cumulative.cumulative.Cumulative.register_transform
documentation: Register a new transform named `name`, implemented by class `cls`.

methods.name: cumulative.cumulative.Cumulative.frame
documentation: Return dataframe with `src` as column prefix.

methods.name: cumulative.cumulative.Cumulative.describe
documentation: Print basic statistics about the collection in the `src` dimension.

functions.name: cumulative.examples.sigmoid.sigmoid
documentation: Animate the normalized tunable Sigmoid function.

functions.name: cumulative.examples.iris.animate
documentation:
Animate the dataset, with multi-effect transitions.
The result is an animation that can be repeated with no
point of discontinuity.

functions.name: cumulative.examples.iris.iris
documentation: Animate Iris dataset.

functions.name: cumulative.examples.randomwalk.randomwalk
documentation: Animate a dataset of random walks.

functions.name: cumulative.examples.sigmoid2.sigmoid2
documentation:
Animate the IRIS dataset, with multi-effect transitions.
The result is an animation that can be repeated with no
point of discontinuity.

functions.name: cumulative.examples.datasaurus.animate
documentation: Animate the Datasaurus dataset, transitioning between different samples.

functions.name: cumulative.opts.options
documentation: Returns singleton object of options.

functions.name: cumulative.datasets.load_betainc.betainc_record
documentation:
Generate a new record representing the betainc series with parameters `a` and `b`
using `num` equidistant points between 0 and 1, with `dst` key prefix.

functions.name: cumulative.datasets.load_betainc.load_betainc
documentation:
Load a synthetic dataset of `size` betainc curves with a and b parameters ranging within
intervals [`a_min`, `a_max`] and [`b_min`, `b_max`], represented by `num` points each.

functions.name: cumulative.datasets.load_ones.load_ones
documentation: Load a dataset of `n` sries of length `m`, filled with ones.

functions.name: cumulative.datasets.load_joydivision.load_joydivision
documentation:
CP 1919 / PSR B1919+21 Dataset

Source:
    https://www.r-bloggers.com/2024/06/cp-1919-psr-b191921-dataset/?ref=dailydev
    https://github.com/pachadotdev/cp1919

functions.name: cumulative.datasets.load_xy.load_xy
documentation: Load a dataset of `n` series of length `m`, x=y in the interval [0,1].

functions.name: cumulative.datasets.load_twoseries.load_twoseries
documentation: Load a dataset of two time series.

functions.name: cumulative.plotting.default_subplots
documentation: Create subplots with a given figsize config via options.

functions.name: cumulative.plotting.plot_notemplate_ctx
documentation:
Prepare a matplotlib plot (single sub-plot):

`x_label`: X label
`y_label`: Y label
`legend`: Dictionary passed to ax.legend(...)
`title`: Title of the plot
`interactive_mode`: set plt.ioff() or plt.ion()
`animation_mode`: if True, sets `interactive_mode` and `show` off
`backend`: Backend to use
`facecolor`: Background color of axis area
`facecolor_fig`: Background color of figure area
`yerr`: If true, report Y error bars
`x_min`: X left limit
`x_max`: X right limit
`y_min`: Y bottom limit
`y_max`: Y top limit
`x_lim`: X limits, passed to ax.set_xlim(...)
`y_lim`: Y limits, passed to ax.set_ylim(...)
`spines_bottomleft`: Show only left,bottom spines
`x_minor_locator`: X Minor locator
`x_major_locator`: X Major locator
`y_minor_locator`: Y Minor locator
`y_major_locator`: Y Major locator
`y_logscale`: If true, set logscale for Y
`y_grid`: If true, show grid on Y
`hatches`: Show hatches on bars
`ax`: If not None, use it as axis object to draw on
`fixsize`: size (x,y) of the subplot
`show`: If true, call plt.show()
`show_axes`: If false, empty white canvas without axes
`savefig_svg`: Save figure to pathname in SVG format
`close`: If true, close figure as last step
`mpl_rc`': matplotlib rc options
`mpl_style`: matplotlib style options

functions.name: cumulative.plotting.plot_ctx
documentation:
Return a context manager generator with options pulled
from a template name `template_name`, and custom ones via `params`.

classes.name: cumulative.plotting.Plot

methods.name: cumulative.plotting.Plot.__init__
documentation: Initializes the plotting interface for the `c` Cumulative instance.

methods.name: cumulative.plotting.Plot.xrays
documentation: Interpolate series on `k` points and render them as monochrome points/curves.

methods.name: cumulative.plotting.Plot.draw
documentation: Basic visualization of a collection of series.

methods.name: cumulative.plotting.Plot.fingerprint
documentation:
Sample and interpolate series on max 100 series, 100 points each, and use the ".z" suffix to select the
color from the default colormap. This visualization is robust to large datasets, as it samples and
simplifies the curves.
This class provides an interface to a curated set of visualization routines.

functions.name: cumulative.datasets.load_dist.dist_record_cdf
documentation:
Generate a new record representing the `dist` distribution CDF
with `num` points and `dst` key prefix.

functions.name: cumulative.datasets.load_dist.dist_record_pdf
documentation:
Generate a new record representing the `dist` distribution PDF
with `num` points and `dst` key prefix.

functions.name: cumulative.datasets.load_dist.dist_record_rvs
documentation:
Generate a new record representing the `dist` distribution
random samples with `num` points and `dst` key prefix.
Points are sorted by ascending order.

functions.name: cumulative.datasets.load_dist.load_dist
documentation:
Load a synthetic dataset representing `dists` curves, with CDF (kind="cdf") or random samples
(kind="rvs"), each with `num` points, with prefix `dst`.

functions.name: cumulative.datasets.load_datasaurus.load_datasaurus
documentation: Load datasaurus dataset, see https://jumpingrivers.github.io/datasauRus/

functions.name: cumulative.datasets.load_ucr.load_ucr2018
documentation:
Load dataset from UCR Archive (2018) by passing `pathdir` as root directory
of the archive and the dataset name `name`.

functions.name: cumulative.datasets.load_sklearn.load_sklearn
documentation: Load a scikit-learn dataset by passing the loading function `func`.

functions.name: cumulative.datasets.load_zeros.load_zeros
documentation: Load a dataset of `n` sries of length `m`, filled with zeros.

functions.name: cumulative.datasets.load_wide.load_wide
documentation: Load a dataset of time series in wide format with numerical and categorical columns.

functions.name: cumulative.transforms.row.diff.diff
documentation:
Return dictionary with diff of `values`, nested in key `prefix`.
Inverse of .cumsum().

methods.name: cumulative.transforms.row.diff.Diff.transform_row
documentation: Apply diff to `kind` dimensions in prefix `src`.

functions.name: cumulative.transforms.row.scale.scale
documentation: Scale `values` to [0,1] range with `prefix` keys.

methods.name: cumulative.transforms.row.scale.Scale.transform_row
documentation: Apply minmax normalization to range [0,1] for `kind` dimensions ("x", "y" or "xy").

functions.name: cumulative.transforms.row.cumsum.cumsum
documentation: Return dictionary with cumulative sum of `values`, nested in key `prefix`.

methods.name: cumulative.transforms.row.cumsum.CumSum.transform_row
documentation:
Apply cumulative sum to `kind` dimension(s) ("x", "y", or "xy") from
prefix `src`.

functions.name: cumulative.transforms.row.bin.bin_values
documentation: Given `values`, bin them in `num` equidistant bins`.

methods.name: cumulative.transforms.row.bin.Bin.transform_row
documentation:
Simplify curve binning Y values on `num` bins. `kind` control the source
of the values: "x", "y", or "xy" (both "x" and "y", independently.)

functions.name: cumulative.transforms.row.interpolate.interpolate_interp
documentation:
Interpolate x,y curve with `k` equidistant points using one-dimensional linear
interpolation for monotonically increasing sample points.

functions.name: cumulative.transforms.row.interpolate.interpolate_pchip
documentation:
PCHIP 1-D monotonic cubic interpolation on x,y curve.
The resulting interpolation passed thru all x,y points.

functions.name: cumulative.transforms.row.interpolate.interpolate_pchipp
documentation:
PCHIP 1-D monotonic cubic interpolation on `k` equi-distant (or `p`) percentiles.
1. First, we fit a PCHIP model with all data points (x,y).
2. We then extract the (x_hat,y_hat) pairs at percentiles x_hat (with k: equi-distant).
3. We fit a new PCHIP model on (x_hat,y_hat), and return it.

functions.name: cumulative.transforms.row.interpolate.interpolate
documentation:
Interpolate x,y curve using `method` and its key arguments, returning a callable
to generate Y values from an X array, and interpolation properties. In case of errors,
an "error" key is also added, with an error message.

methods.name: cumulative.transforms.row.interpolate.Interpolate.transform_row
documentation: Interpolate  x,y arrays with prefix `src` using `method` and `num` data points.

functions.name: cumulative.transforms.row.row_apply.start_zero
documentation: Insert [0,0] as first pair for series x,y

functions.name: cumulative.transforms.row.row_apply.last_x_nonzero_y
documentation:
Sets `col` to the last value in x with a nonzero value on y,
useful to trim ending zeros on y.

functions.name: cumulative.transforms.row.row_apply.limit_region
documentation: Limit x,y to a region [x_min,x_max] and [y_min,y_max]

methods.name: cumulative.transforms.row.row_apply.RowApply.transform_row
documentation: Apply `func` to `row`, with `src` column prefix omitted to increase reusability.

functions.name: cumulative.transforms.row.morph.morph
documentation: Morph `a` to `b` by a percentage `pct` with `prefix` prefix.

methods.name: cumulative.transforms.row.morph.Morph.transform_row
documentation: Morph `src` to `src2` by a percentage. Series length must be equal.

methods.name: cumulative.transforms.frame.sample.Sample.apply
documentation:
Sample dataset, either with a fixed number `n` of rows, or a
percentage `frac`. If `m` is provided, up to `m` rows are considered.

functions.name: cumulative.transforms.row.template.normalized_tunable_sigmoid
documentation:
Normalized tunable sigmoid function, with k in [-1,1].
https://dhemery.github.io/DHE-Modules/technical/sigmoid/

methods.name: cumulative.transforms.row.template.Template.transform_row
documentation: Construct a templated series of type `name` from `src`.

methods.name: cumulative.transforms.frame.score.Score.apply
documentation:
Extract a score from `src` column using `method`.
Useful for sorting and setting colors.

methods.name: cumulative.transforms.frame.cluster.Cluster.apply
documentation: At each `x` position, cluster `y` values with k-means.

methods.name: cumulative.transforms.frame.frame_apply.FrameApply.apply
documentation: Apply `func` to the frame.

methods.name: cumulative.transforms.frame.sort.Sort.apply
documentation:
Sort rows in data frame based on column `by`, honoring
`ascending` option. The order of the rows is affected.
No new columns are created.

methods.name: cumulative.transforms.frame.copy.Copy.apply
documentation: Copy columns with `src` prefix (destination handled in transforms.py)

methods.name: cumulative.transforms.frame.drop.Drop.apply
documentation: Drop columns with prefix `src`

classes.name: cumulative.transforms.transform.Transform

methods.name: cumulative.transforms.transform.Transform.__init__
documentation: Link transform to Cumulative class and set its name.

methods.name: cumulative.transforms.transform.Transform.transform_row
documentation:
Transformations can be applied either on rows or columns.
For rows, the `transform_row` method is overloaded and it
returns a series with the new columns to add.

methods.name: cumulative.transforms.transform.Transform.__call__
documentation: Handles transform requests.

methods.name: cumulative.transforms.transform.Transform.apply
documentation:
Transformations can be applied either on rows or columns.
For columns, the `apply` method is overloaded and it
returns a data frame with the new columns to add.

By default, it handles row transforms.
Abstraction of transforms.

functions.name: cumulative.utils.aggregates.get_minmax
documentation:
Get min, max values for X/Y dimensions. `expand` is added as
a percentage increase to increase the covered area.

methods.name: cumulative.utils.validate.Validate.check
documentation:
Verify that linked cumulative data frame doesn't countain nans or infs,
that there are both rows and columns.
If `deep` is True, also arrays within cells are checked.
If issues are detected, a warning is generated, with `prefix` string.

functions.name: cumulative.utils.warnings.warn
documentation: Utility function to handle warnings.

functions.name: cumulative.utils.frames.columns_with_prefix
documentation:
Return list of columns in `df` dataframe with `prefix`. If no matches and errors == "raise",
raise NoMatchingColumns exception.

functions.name: cumulative.utils.frames.rows_with_prefix
documentation:
Return list of index values in `s` series with `prefix`. If no matches and errors == "raise",
raise NoMatchingColumns exception.

functions.name: cumulative.utils.frames.drop_cols_with_prefix
documentation:
Drop columns with `prefix` from `df` dataframe and return it.
If no matches and errors == "raise", NoMatchingColumns is raised.

functions.name: cumulative.utils.lineage.get_kwargs
documentation: Return kwargs passed to funcion calling get_kwargs().

classes.name: cumulative.utils.lineage.Lineage

methods.name: cumulative.utils.lineage.Lineage.track
documentation: Track transform call `name` to `dst` with `kwargs`.

methods.name: cumulative.utils.lineage.Lineage.clear
documentation: Clear the currently tracked history.

methods.name: cumulative.utils.lineage.Lineage.explain
documentation: Print operations applied to data frame.
Handling of lineage of operations.
